rationale_for_baseline_tier: Acute bilateral masseter spasm/trismus with referred pain, and a negative screen for airway/deep-space infection, GCA and dislocation, is myofascial TMD — not an ED-by-ambulance emergency — but the mouth/jaw can't be examined remotely and trismus limits intake, so urgent in-person dental/GP review plus supportive care and hydration (T3) is proportionate. T2 is the minimum viable pass. Escalate to T5 for neck/chin swelling with breathing difficulty, sudden vision loss, or a jaw stuck open.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Swelling under the chin/neck with drooling or difficulty breathing/swallowing saliva
escalation_edges.rationale: Indicates a deep-space (Ludwig-type) infection threatening the airway.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you develop swelling under your chin or neck with drooling or trouble breathing, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden vision change, new scalp tenderness/jaw claudication, or a focal dental abscess with high fever
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Seek urgent care / ED (emergency eye service for vision change)
escalation_edges.rationale: Would indicate GCA (vision-threatening) or a spreading dental infection.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your vision suddenly changes, your scalp/temples become tender with pain on chewing that eases with rest, or you get a hot swollen tooth/gum with fever — seek urgent care (and an emergency eye service for vision change).

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Jaw becomes stuck open and can't close, or you can't keep any fluids down
escalation_edges.rationale: A fixed dislocation needs reduction; dehydration needs assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your jaw gets stuck open and won't close, or you can't keep any fluids down, get seen the same day.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Ensure hydration/soft-liquid nutrition and arrange the in-person review; check someone can help.
Amplifies pain; manage with supportive measures and GP-led adjuncts rather than remote sedatives.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Your jaw muscles have cramped up from grinding, which locks the jaw and shoots pain to your temples — a jaw-muscle (TMD) problem worsened by your fibromyalgia. It's very unlikely to be dangerous given your normal breathing and no neck swelling, but you should be seen in person soon (dentist/GP). Use warm compresses, rest the jaw, take soft/liquid meals and sips of fluid, and simple painkillers within limits.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent in-person dental/GP review within 1–2 days; use 000/urgent care for the features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Call 000 for neck/chin swelling with breathing difficulty, seek an emergency eye service for vision change, and same-day care for a jaw stuck open or inability to keep fluids down.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note the acute bilateral masseter spasm/trismus after bruxism, negative airway/GCA/dislocation screen, reduced intake, and the need for a jaw/oral examination.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Managing/reassuring acute trismus with no airway/deep-space-infection screen, missing a Ludwig-type infection or GCA.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: A deep-space infection progresses to airway obstruction, or GCA-related vision loss is missed.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance/000 for uncomplicated myofascial trismus with a clean danger-sign screen, over-medicalising a manageable problem.
